name: Tony (Tongyun) Yang
tagline: mean the limits of the world?
in more than we can name.
heroSpecialtyLine: Years across AI, from LLM routing to medical imaging. Now I build in two places, and I know exactly why.
openingHeadline: I spent years moving across AI, from LLM routing to medical imaging. Then I left a fellowship on purpose, because I finally knew what I wanted to build.
openingBody: The breadth was not indecision, it was reconnaissance. It showed me where the work that matters actually lives, and where I can do it best. So I converged on two: building where data is sensitive and scarce, and turning frontier models into systems people can actually use.
currently: An independent AI researcher in Amsterdam. I currently collaborate with industry (Tencent, Gradient Networks, MeetaVista) and academia (TU Delft, McGill, Tsinghua) on cost-efficient large language models, optimization-as-reasoning, and intent-aware world models. Earlier roles include Marie Skłodowska-Curie Fellow, AI Research Engineer at TU Delft Imaging Physics, and an MSc degree from TU Delft.
intro: Open to research, in academia or industry. What pulls me is meaning more than topic.

diptych.heading: Two directions
diptych.thesis: Two bets, one belief: build where data is sensitive and scarce, and turn frontier models into products people can actually use.
diptych.belief: I believe these two pave the next paradigm of AI.

directions.title: When the data doesn't exist, I build it
directions.blurb: The work that matters most happens where public data barely reaches and mistakes are costly: the clinic, the headset, the device, the closed enterprise. Reverse Imaging segments cardiac MRI it has never seen. Pruning nnU-Net removes over 80% of a medical network with quality intact. Through the Eyes of Emotion is the first public VR eye-tracking dataset, built because none existed. This is AI for the people a leaderboard forgets.

directions.title: Frontier capability, made cheap enough to ship
directions.blurb: Frontier models are astonishing, and almost no one can afford to run them well. MERA holds 87.3% router accuracy at 51.8% of always-large-model cost. TwinRouterBench matches unrouted Opus 4.6 on SWE-bench Verified for 53% less. The LLM Router cut cost by over 90% at the same quality. Capable is the start. Useful is the work.

diptych.breadthLine: On the ground beneath both: a wide spectrum of AI, from LLM routing to medical imaging, across Tencent, Gradient Networks, MeetaVista, TU Delft, McGill, Tsinghua, and IMDEA. The range is what earned the convergence.

researchDek: The receipts behind the two directions. Every paper here is one of those bets made concrete.
projectsIntro: Where the research meets real systems. Some are live collaborations (Gradient Networks, Tencent, MeetaVista); others I build on my own time. Most are still in motion.

businessCard.name: Tony Yang
businessCard.looking: Open to research · academia or industry
businessCard.tagline: Sensitive, scarce-data AI · frontier models made shippable

contactInvitation: I read everything. Reach out about research, collaboration, supervision, or just to say hi. I answer faster than is professional, and I bring my whole self to the conversation.
